info.title: Podbul Public API
info.description: Public surface endpoints with ApiResult wrapper responses

post.description:
This endpoint validates the provided API credentials and returns a JWT token upon successful authentication.
The token should be included in the Authorization header as "Bearer {token}" for subsequent API calls.

**Authentication Process:**
1. Validates API key and secret are provided
2. Looks up user credentials in the database 
3. Generates and returns a JWT token with user claims

**Security Notes:**
- API secrets are hashed using unique salts
- Tokens have configurable expiration times
- Failed authentication attempts are logged for security monitoring

requestBody.description: Authentication request containing API key and secret

400.description: API key or secret is missing or empty

post.summary: Product List with Full Details and Filtering
post.description:
This endpoint provides access to the complete product catalog with rich filtering and pagination capabilities.
Products are returned with all associated metadata required for display and ordering.

**Product Information Included:**
- Basic product details (name, description, SKU)
- Available colors and color variations
- Size options and size charts
- Category classifications and hierarchy
- Product mockups and preview images
- Asset parameters for customization
- Pricing tables and cost information
- Availability and stock status

**Filtering and Pagination:**
- Category filtering is mandatory for performance
- Search functionality across product names and descriptions
- Pagination with configurable page sizes (max 200 items)
- Only published products are returned by default

**Performance Considerations:**
- Applied filters are processed in batch queries to prevent N+1 issues
- Related tables are fetched in single operations for consistent performance
- Query count remains constant even on high page numbers

**Business Rules:**
- Only products with PublishState=Publish are returned
- Price information shows the minimum price from PriceTables
- Category ID must be obtained from the /categories endpoint
- All monetary values are in the base currency (USD)

**Print Area Information:**
- 0 = Front
- 1 = Back
- 2 = Front and Back
- 3 = Front Pocket
- 4 = Front Pocket and Back

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items per page, maximum 200 recommended (default: 20)

requestBody.description: Filtering criteria including required category ID and optional search parameters

200.description: Products retrieved successfully with pagination metadata

500.description: Internal server error during product retrieval

get.description:
Returns the cheapest carrier base shipping rate per country for a given product.
The endpoint calculates shipping costs based on the product's minimum weight across all sizes.
            
**Caching:**
- Results are cached for 1 hour per product to improve performance
            
**Common Error Scenarios:**
- Product has no sizes defined → 404
- Product sizes have no weight information → 404
- No carrier base rate matches the product weight → 404

200.description: Base rates retrieved successfully with per-country cheapest carrier details

404.description: No sizes, weights, or matching base rates found for the product

get.description:
This endpoint returns the entire category taxonomy organized in a hierarchical tree format.
Categories are structured with parent-child relationships to enable navigation and filtering.
 
**Category Tree Structure:**
- Root categories (parent categories with no parent)
- Child categories nested under their respective parents
- Multiple levels of nesting supported
- Each category includes ID, name, description, and child count

**Use Cases:**
- Building navigation menus for product browsing
- Category selection for product filtering
- Hierarchical category displays in user interfaces
- SEO-friendly URL generation with category paths

**Performance Notes:**
- Tree structure is optimized for fast retrieval
- Categories are cached for improved response times
- Includes only active/published categories
- Minimal database queries using efficient tree traversal

**Data Format:**
- Each category node contains: ID, Name, Description, ParentID
- Children array contains nested subcategories
- Leaf nodes have empty children arrays
- Product counts may be included per category

200.description: Category tree retrieved successfully with complete hierarchy

500.description: Internal server error during category tree retrieval

post.description:
This endpoint allows authenticated users to create new orders in the system.
The order will be created with a unique identifier and initial status.

**Order Creation Process:**
1. Validates user authentication and authorization
2. Creates a new order entity with provided details
3. Assigns a unique order ID and timestamp
4. Sets initial order status to "Created"
5. Stores order in MongoDB collection

**Business Rules:**
- Only authenticated users can create orders
- User ID is extracted from JWT token
- Order details are validated before creation
- All orders are logged for audit purposes

**Print Area Information:**
- 0 = Front
- 1 = Back
- 2 = Front and Back
- 3 = Front Pocket
- 4 = Front Pocket and Back

**ImageUrl1 and ImageUrl2 Usage:**
- **ImageUrl1** and **ImageUrl2** are the mockup image URLs used for printing on the product.
- **Accessibility:** The provided image URLs must be publicly accessible over the internet.
  The system downloads these images via HTTP GET requests and re-uploads them to internal storage,
  so any URL that requires authentication or is behind a firewall will cause the order to fail.
- **Resolution:** Images should be **300 DPI** for optimal print quality.
  If a lower resolution image is provided, the system will automatically upscale and convert it to 300 DPI,
  though this may affect the final print quality.

requestBody.description: Order creation payload containing order details and items

201.description: Order created successfully - returns order ID

400.description: Invalid user ID or malformed request data

500.description: Internal server error during order creation

get.description:
This endpoint allows authenticated users to fetch details of their own orders.
Only orders belonging to the authenticated user can be retrieved for security.

**Order Retrieval Process:**
1. Validates user authentication from JWT token
2. Searches for order by ID and user ownership
3. Returns complete order details if found
4. Includes order items, status, and metadata

**Security Notes:**
- Users can only access their own orders
- Order ID must be a valid MongoDB ObjectId or Podbul Order ID
- All access attempts are logged for security monitoring

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique Podbul Order ID (MongoDB ObjectId format)

200.description: Order retrieved successfully with complete details

404.description: Order not found or user doesn't have access

400.description: Invalid user ID extracted from authentication token

500.description: Internal server error during order retrieval

post.description:
This endpoint allows authenticated users to cancel their own orders.
Only orders that are in a cancellable state can be cancelled.

**Order Cancellation Process:**
1. Validates user authentication and order ownership
2. Checks if order is in a cancellable state
3. Updates order status to "Cancelled"
4. Records cancellation timestamp and user
5. Logs cancellation for audit purposes

**Business Rules:**
- Only order owners can cancel their orders
- Orders in certain states (e.g., "Shipped") cannot be cancelled
- Cancellation actions are irreversible
- Refund processing may be initiated separately

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique Podbul Order ID to be cancelled

202.description: Order cancellation accepted and processed successfully

404.description: Order not found or user doesn't have cancellation rights

400.description: Invalid user ID extracted from authentication token

500.description: Internal server error during order cancellation

put.summary: Updates the shipping address of an existing order
put.description:
This endpoint allows authenticated users to update the shipping address of their own orders.
Only orders that have not yet entered the fulfillment pipeline can be updated.
            
**Business Rules:**
- Only the order owner can update the shipping address
- Orders that are Preparing, Shipped, Delivered, or Cancelled cannot be updated
- Address fields (Name, Street1, City, State, PostalCode, Country) are required
- Country must be a valid ISO-2 or recognized country value

400.description: Invalid user ID or address validation failed

500.description: Internal server error during address update

get.summary: Gets available shipping rates for an existing order
get.description:
This endpoint queries available carrier shipping rates for the given order.
The order must have a valid shipping address configured before rates can be retrieved.
            
**Rate Validity:**
- Retrieved shipping rates are valid for **240 minutes (4 hours)** from the time of retrieval.
- After this period, the rates expire and are no longer accurate.
- If the rates have expired, this endpoint must be called again to obtain up-to-date pricing before submitting the order.
- Submitting an order with expired rates may result in a rejection or a rate mismatch error.
            
**Recommended Flow:**
1. Call this endpoint to retrieve current rates.
2. Present the available options to the user.
3. Ensure the user selects a rate and the order is submitted **within 240 minutes**.
4. If the window has passed, call this endpoint again before proceeding with submission.

404.description: Order not found or user doesn't have access

400.description: Invalid user ID or order has no shipping address

500.description: Internal server error during rate retrieval

get.description:
This endpoint manages user-specific wallet and payment preferences within the Podbul platform.
If the user doesn't have existing preferences, default settings will be automatically created.

**Wallet Preference Features:**
- Default payment methods and priorities
- Currency preferences for transactions
- Billing address and payment profiles
- Auto-payment settings and configurations
- Transaction limits and spending controls

**Automatic Creation:**
- New users get default preference settings
- Preferences are created with system defaults
- User can modify preferences after creation
- All changes are tracked for audit purposes

**Security Considerations:**
- User authentication required via JWT token
- Users can only access their own preferences
- Sensitive payment data is properly secured
- All access is logged for security monitoring

**Usage Patterns:**
- Called during user onboarding process
- Retrieved before payment processing
- Updated when user modifies payment settings
- Integrated with order creation workflows

200.description: Wallet preferences retrieved successfully, created if not existing

401.description: User authentication failed or invalid token

500.description: Internal server error during preference retrieval or creation

categoryId.description: Required category Id. If not sent, empty result will be returned.
